channel.title: Comments for page "Why is Five afraid of Seven?"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Why is Five afraid of Seven?"

item.title: So 2747 is a God?
item.content:encoded:
Hm… an interesting take, but 2747 always seemed to me to be more akin to a narrative force than any sort of God, but 2747 comes in many forms. I guess my interpretation would be that this God with Seven horns is but one manifestation of 2747 out many in the narrative stack.

Also, I am guessing we are supposed to conclude that they were all mutually wiped by this Goddess manifestation of 2747 right? Probably what would happen to people who try to worship a goddamn anti-narrative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read it all. I'm a much bigger fan1 of 5thists than other fictional religions.
+1

Footnotes
1. Not to say I'm pro-5thist, just see them as well made antagonists

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do we have to look out for seventhists eatining ninthists now?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not really into this, largely due to personal foibles: The imagery is cool, but grand high fantasy religious content isn't really my thing.

I do think combining the two SCPs in question and the antagonist of the antimemetics tales is brilliant, and I'm glad you're done so. It just happens that as we discussed in chat, you have a different interpretation of fifthism than the original SCP, which is fine (I'm not big on the Scientology angle either), but you've taken it in a direction that doesn't do much for me.

So, cool imagery, very cool idea, but no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I loved it in the chat, and I love it here.

+1

Also SCP-3125 x SCP-2747 is new ship

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A New Challenger Approaches: Lord of the Non-Existent!

This is cool as heck, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ohey, I remember seeing this on chat. Good tale, nice imagery, more god shipping for the shipping throne. Very nice.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ha! Are the fifthists and the entity from SCP-2747 the next CotBGxSarkcism? Only time will tell.

While this is a brief tale, it has great imagery and does a lot of world building. Additionally, the SCP agents at the end commenting on the "Why is Five afraid of Seven?" note is pretty humorous.

+1. You crazy kids and your shipping of eldritch horrors.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wished I could have been upvote #7, but still. Great job of setting the scene, as I could picture it perfectly. Tying those two skips together was a genius move.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great tale.
I really enjoyed it, it was really unique, with hints of humour.

This leaves me with the only spot of a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seven cult? Never… Er… never heard of it! Heheh, heh… Heh? :3
(Looks sheepishly at username, and upvotes)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This tale is based on old version of SCP-2454 and SCP-2747, and used some of the fifthist god headcanon from qntm's Antimemetic series. I do recommend reading both skips before reading the tale to get the context.

Special thanks to Metaphysician and minmin for approving of and supporting my re-interpretation of their skips. Aslo thanks to AidenEldritch, nox, TwistedGears, AetherialSkies, Lex1nat0r, SleepLight, NineVolt, shaggydredlocks, Roget, Petrograd, Conwell, Gaffsey, Kieraaa and others if I unfortunately left out any for reviewing the draft :3

Actually an excuse for more god shipping :P
